zh.Chelmsford, Essex, England: 英國艾可傑爾姆斯福德
zh.Writtle, Springfield, Essex, England: 英國英格蘭艾可萊特爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Henry George於1585年2月出生在英國艾可萊特爾時，他的父親Richard Henry George21歲，母親Anne Gibson21歲。他於1602年5月與Anne Greene在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1612年5月16日在英國英格蘭艾可萊特爾享年27歲，埋葬在英國艾可萊特爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Henry George于1585年2月出生在英国艾可萊特爾时，他的父亲Richard Henry George21岁，母亲Anne Gibson21岁。他于1602年5月与Anne Greene在英国艾可结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1612年5月16日在英国英格兰艾可萊特爾享年27岁，埋葬在英国艾可萊特爾。

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Welsh, French, and Romanian: from the personal name George, Latin Georgius, Greek Geōrgios, from an adjectival form, geōrgios ‘rustic’, of Greek geōrgos ‘farmer’. This became established as a personal name in classical times through its association with the fashion for pastoral poetry. Its popularity in western Europe increased at the time of the Crusades, which brought greater contact with the Orthodox Church, in which several Christian martyrs and saints of this name are venerated, in particular a saint believed to have been martyred at Nicomedia in AD 303, who, however, is at best a shadowy figure historically. Nevertheless, by the end of the Middle Ages Saint George had become associated with an unhistorical legend of dragon-slaying exploits, which caught the popular imagination throughout Europe, and he came to be considered the patron saint of England among other places. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Georg , Assyrian/Chaldean Giwargis, Gewargis , or Georgis , and Albanian Gjergji , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Georgiadis , Georgopoulos , Hatzigeorgiou ‘George the Pilgrim’, and Papageorgiou , Romanian Georgescu or Gheorghescu, Serbian Djordjevic . The name George is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Geevarghese and Varghese ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name George (see 1 above) as a surname.

variants.name: Djordjevic
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Serbian or Croatian Dragan, Bozidar, Branko, Milan, Miodrag, Momcilo, Predrag, Sinisa, Sotir, Borka, Djordje, Dragana, Dunja, Dusan, Mirko, Miroslav, Milutin, Misha, Vladimir.

Serbian and Croatian (Djordjević, standard spelling Đorđević): patronymic from the especially Serbian personal name Đorđe, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ). Đorđević is the fifth most frequent surname in Serbia. Compare Georgevich .

variants.name: Djukic
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Djukić, standard spelling Đukić): patronymic from a pet form of the personal name Đurađ, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ).

variants.name: Durko
meanings: Slovak (Ďurko): from the personal name Ďurko, a pet form of Juraj, an equivalent of George . Compare Darko .

variants.name: Gazzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Almando, Attilio, Julio, Salvatore.

Italian: probably from the medieval Greek personal name Gatsos, a pet form of Georgatsos, a derivative of Geōrgios (see George ). Alternatively, a nickname from Sicilian gazzu ‘blue-eyed’ (also ‘short-sighted’), or from Old Italian gazzo ‘deep blue’ (like the feathers of a magpie, Italian gazza). It could also be a habitational name from Gazzo in Padova province, Gazzo Veronese in Verona province, or from a shortened form of Agazzo, from Late Latin Agathius, either a masculine form of Agata or from the analogous ancient Germanic personal name Agathio.

variants.name: Geevarghese
meanings: Indian (Kerala): from the personal name Geevarghese, a derivative of Greek Geōrgios (see George ), found among Christians in southern India. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Ganta
meanings: Indian (Andhra Pradesh): name borne by a sept of Kamma (agrarian) and Balija (trader) people, from Telugu ganta ‘bell’.
Romanian (Ganţă): from a derivative of the personal name Gană, Gane, Ganu, of Bulgarian origin (a pet form of local names corresponding to George ).

variants.name: Durica
meanings: Slovak and Czech (Moravian) (Ďurica): patronymic from the personal name Ďuro, a vernacular form of local equivalents (Juraj, Jura) of Latin Georgius (see George ).

variants.name: Djuric
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Djurić, standard spelling Đurić): patronymic from the personal names Đura and Đuro, short forms of Đurađ, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ). See also Juric , compare Duric .

variants.name: Georg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Gerd, Kurt, Manfred.
German: from the personal name Georg, an equivalent of Latin Georgius (see George ).

variants.name: Djokic
meanings: Serbian (Djokić, standard spelling Đokić): patronymic from a pet form of the personal name Đorđe, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When William Henry George was born in February 1585, in Writtle, Essex, England, his father, Richard Henry George, was 21 and his mother, Anne Gibson, was 21. He married Anne Greene in May 1602, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 16 May 1612, in Writtle, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 27, and was buried in Writtle, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Henry George est né en février 1585, à Writtle, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Richard Henry George, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Anne Gibson, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Anne Greene en mai 1602, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 16 mai 1612, à Writtle, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 27 ans, et a été enterré à Writtle, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Henry George im Februar 1585 in Writtle, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Henry George 21 und seine Mutter Anne Gibson 21. Er hat im Mai 1602 in Essex, England Anne Greene geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 16. Mai 1612 im Alter von 27 Jahren in Writtle, Essex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Writtle, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Henry George è nato il nel febbraio 1585 nel Writtle, Essex, England, il suo padre, Richard Henry George, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Anne Gibson, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Anne Greene nel maggio 1602, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 16 maggio 1612, nel Writtle, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 27 anni, e fu sepolto a Writtle, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Henry Georgeが1585年2月にイギリスエセックスウリットルで生まれた時，父Richard Henry Georgeは21歳，母Anne Gibsonは21歳であった。1602年5月にイギリスエセックスでAnne Greeneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1612年5月16日にイギリスイギリスエセックスウリットルで27歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスウリットルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Henry George(이)가 1585년 2월 영국 에세스 리틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Henry George(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Gibson(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1602년 5월 영국 에세스에서 Anne Greene(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1612년 5월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 리틀에서 27세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 리틀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Henry George nasceu em fevereiro de 1585, em Writtle, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Henry George, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Anne Gibson, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Greene em maio de 1602, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 16 de maio de 1612, em Writtle, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 27 anos, e foi sepultado em Writtle, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Henry George родился февраль 1585 г. в местности риттл, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Henry George было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Anne Gibson, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Greene май 1602 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 16 мая 1612 г. в местности риттл, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 27 лет и был похоронен в местности риттл, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Henry George nació en febrero de 1585, en Writtle, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Henry George, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Anne Gibson, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Anne Greene en mayo de 1602, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 16 de mayo de 1612, en Writtle, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 27 años, y fue sepultado en Writtle, Essex, Inglaterra.
